annotations.label: Finite field

anchor.snippet: a finite field or Galois field

annotations.note: Mathlib encodes finite fields via the combination of the typeclasses `[Field K] [Finite K]` (or `[Fintype K]`); there is no dedicated `FiniteField` definition decl, but the `FiniteField` namespace in `Mathlib/FieldTheory/Finite/Basic.lean` collects all results under this assumption.

annotations.label: Order of a finite field

anchor.snippet: The order of a finite field is its number of elements

annotations.note: The order is the cardinality `Fintype.card K` (alias `Nat.card K`); throughout `FiniteField` results it is abbreviated `q`.

annotations.label: Existence and isomorphism of finite fields by order

anchor.snippet: For every prime number

annotations.note: Existence is given by `GaloisField p n` with `GaloisField.card`, and uniqueness up to isomorphism by `FiniteField.algEquivOfCardEq` / `FiniteField.ringEquivOfCardEq`.

annotations.label: Order/size of a finite field (Properties)

anchor.snippet: The number of elements of a finite field is called its order

annotations.note: Same notion as in the lead: `Fintype.card K` (notation `q`).

annotations.label: Characterization of orders of finite fields

anchor.snippet: A finite field of order

annotations.note: `FiniteField.card` (with `FiniteField.card'` and `FiniteField.isPrimePow_card`) states that any finite field has cardinality a prime power.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of finite fields of given order

anchor.snippet: all fields of order

annotations.note: Any two finite fields of equal cardinality are ring-isomorphic via `FiniteField.ringEquivOfCardEq` (and algebra-isomorphic via `FiniteField.algEquivOfCardEq`).

annotations.label: Polynomial X^q - X has all elements as roots

anchor.snippet: the polynomial

annotations.note: `FiniteField.pow_card` shows `a ^ q = a` for all `a : K`; `FiniteField.roots_X_pow_card_sub_X` packages this as a roots-multiset equality.

annotations.label: Multiplicative group of a finite field is cyclic

anchor.snippet: The non-zero elements of a finite field form a multiplicative group

annotations.note: The general result `IsCyclic Rˣ` for finite integral domains (`isCyclic_of_subgroup_isDomain` and the `instance [Finite Rˣ] : IsCyclic Rˣ`) covers finite fields.

annotations.label: Primitive element

anchor.snippet: a primitive element of the field

annotations.note: Mathlib expresses primitive elements via `IsCyclic.exists_generator` on `Kˣ`; there is no dedicated `primitiveElement` name for finite fields.

annotations.label: Prime fields as integers modulo p

anchor.snippet: The simplest examples of finite fields are the fields of prime order

annotations.note: `ZMod p` is a field when `p` is prime (via `ZMod.instField`), giving the prime fields.

annotations.label: Characteristic of a finite field

anchor.snippet: The least positive

annotations.note: The characteristic is the general `CharP K p` typeclass; for finite fields it is the prime `p` from `FiniteField.card`.

annotations.label: Freshman's dream identity

anchor.snippet: sometimes called the freshman's dream

annotations.note: `add_pow_char` (and `add_pow_expChar_pow`) gives `(x + y)^p = x^p + y^p` in characteristic `p`.

annotations.label: Fermat's little theorem for finite fields

anchor.snippet: By Fermat's little theorem

annotations.note: `FiniteField.pow_card_sub_one_eq_one` states `a^(q-1) = 1` for nonzero `a`; `ZMod.pow_card_sub_one_eq_one` is the prime-field case.

annotations.label: Finite fields are perfect (separable simple extensions)

anchor.snippet: Any finite field extension of a finite field is separable and simple

annotations.note: `PerfectField.ofFinite` makes every finite field perfect (hence every algebraic extension separable), and `Field.exists_primitive_element` then yields simplicity for finite separable extensions.

annotations.label: Chevalley–Warning: finite fields are quasi-algebraically closed

anchor.snippet: Finite fields are quasi-algebraically closed

annotations.note: Chevalley–Warning itself is formalized as `char_dvd_card_solutions_of_sum_lt`, but the abstract notion of a quasi-algebraically closed (C₁) field is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Splitting field of X^q - X has order q

anchor.snippet: be the splitting field of the polynomial

annotations.note: `GaloisField p n := SplittingField (X^p^n - X)` and `GaloisField.card` proves it has cardinality `p^n`.

anchors.snippet: be the splitting field of the polynomial

annotations.label: Uniqueness up to isomorphism via splitting fields

anchor.snippet: The uniqueness up to isomorphism of splitting fields

annotations.note: `GaloisField.algEquivGaloisField` (with `FiniteField.algEquivOfCardEq`) realizes this via the universal property of the splitting field.

annotations.label: Moore's classification theorem for finite fields

anchor.snippet: The order of a finite field is a prime power

annotations.note: `Fintype.nonempty_field_iff` says a finite type admits a field structure iff its cardinality is a prime power, combining `FiniteField.card` with `GaloisField` existence.

anchors.snippet: The order of a finite field is a prime power

annotations.label: Subfield criterion for finite fields

anchor.snippet: contains a subfield isomorphic to

annotations.note: `FiniteField.nonempty_algHom_iff_finrank_dvd` gives the divisibility criterion for embedding finite-field extensions, but the direct `GF(p^m) ↪ GF(p^n) ↔ m ∣ n` statement is not isolated.

annotations.label: Construction of GF(p^n) via irreducible polynomial quotient

anchor.snippet: the field

annotations.note: Mathlib has the general quotient-by-irreducible construction `AdjoinRoot`, but no dedicated `GF(p^n) := (ZMod p)[X] / (f)` definition.

annotations.label: Field with four elements GF(4)

anchor.snippet: The smallest non-prime field is the field with four elements

annotations.note: Available abstractly as `GaloisField 2 2`, but no explicit small construction or addition/multiplication table is stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Frobenius automorphism on GF(4)

anchor.snippet: is the non-trivial field automorphism

annotations.note: The generic `frobenius` ring hom exists but no instance is specialized as the nontrivial automorphism of `GaloisField 2 2`.

annotations.note: Mathlib has `GaloisField p 2` abstractly but not the explicit `(ZMod p)[X]/(X^2 - r)` presentation.

annotations.label: Construction of GF(16)

anchor.snippet: The polynomial

annotations.note: An explicit construction of `GF(16)` via `X^4 + X + 1` is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Primitive elements of GF(16)

anchor.snippet: has eight primitive elements

annotations.note: No statement enumerating the eight primitive elements of `GF(16)` was found.

annotations.label: Multiplicative group of a finite field is cyclic

anchor.snippet: The multiplicative group of the non-zero elements

annotations.note: Same as the earlier entry: `IsCyclic Kˣ` holds for any finite integral domain via `isCyclic_of_subgroup_isDomain`.

annotations.label: Primitive element of GF(q)

anchor.snippet: is called a primitive element of

annotations.note: Generators of the cyclic group `Kˣ` provide primitive elements via `IsCyclic.exists_generator`; no dedicated finite-field definition.

annotations.label: Number of primitive elements via Euler's totient

anchor.snippet: The number of primitive elements

annotations.note: `IsCyclic.card_orderOf_eq_totient` gives the count of elements of given order in a cyclic group as Euler's totient, applied to `Kˣ` of order `q-1`.

annotations.label: Discrete logarithm

anchor.snippet: is called the discrete logarithm of

annotations.note: No notion of discrete logarithm in `Kˣ` of a finite field was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Zech's logarithms

anchor.snippet: called Zech's logarithms

annotations.note: Zech's logarithms are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Every nonzero element is a root of unity

anchor.snippet: Every nonzero element of a finite field is a root of unity

annotations.note: `FiniteField.pow_card_sub_one_eq_one` shows every nonzero `a : K` satisfies `a^(q-1) = 1`, i.e. is a `(q-1)`-th root of unity.

annotations.label: n-th primitive root of unity

anchor.snippet: th primitive root of unity is a solution of the equation

annotations.note: Mathlib's general `IsPrimitiveRoot ζ n` captures this concept in any monoid.

annotations.label: Existence criterion for primitive n-th roots of unity in GF(q)

anchor.snippet: th primitive root of unity if and only if

annotations.note: Follows from cyclicity of `Kˣ` and `IsCyclic.card_orderOf_eq_totient` together with `Fintype.card_units = q - 1`, but no dedicated `∃ ζ, IsPrimitiveRoot ζ n ↔ n ∣ q-1` is isolated.

annotations.label: No primitive p-th roots of unity in characteristic p

anchor.snippet: primitive

annotations.note: Follows from `a^p = a` (`ZMod.pow_card`/`FiniteField.pow_card`) and `ZMod.frobenius_zmod`, but no isolated statement that there are no primitive `p`-th roots of unity in characteristic `p` was found.

annotations.label: Cyclotomic polynomial factorization in characteristic p

anchor.snippet: the roots of the

annotations.note: Mathlib defines cyclotomic polynomials and proves `prod_cyclotomic_eq_X_pow_sub_one`, but the explicit characteristic-`p` factorization statement is not isolated.

annotations.label: GF(64) subfields and primitive elements

anchor.snippet: The field GF(64) has several interesting properties

annotations.note: No specific worked example about `GF(64)` was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Frobenius automorphism

anchor.snippet: It is called the Frobenius automorphism

annotations.note: `frobenius R p` is the `p`-th power ring endomorphism; `FiniteField.frobeniusAlgEquiv` packages it as an algebra automorphism for finite fields.

anchors.snippet: It is called the Frobenius automorphism

annotations.label: Powers of Frobenius and order n

anchor.snippet: the composition of φ with itself

annotations.note: `FiniteField.frobenius_pow` shows that `frobenius K p ^ n = 1` when `q = p^n`; `FiniteField.orderOf_frobeniusAlgHom` gives the exact order.

anchors.snippet: the composition of φ with itself

annotations.label: Galois group of GF(p^n)/GF(p) is cyclic of order n

anchor.snippet: There are no other GF( p ) -automorphisms

annotations.note: Combined with the `IsCyclic Gal(L/K)` instance for finite extensions of finite fields and `IsGalois.card_aut_eq_finrank`, this gives the full statement.

anchors.snippet: There are no other GF( p ) -automorphisms

annotations.label: GF(p^n) is a Galois extension of GF(p) with cyclic Galois group

anchor.snippet: is a Galois extension of

annotations.note: `GaloisField.instIsGaloisOfFinite` provides an `IsGalois K K'` instance for any finite extension between finite fields, and the cyclic-Galois-group instance for finite extensions of finite fields supplies cyclicity.

annotations.label: Every finite field is perfect (via surjective Frobenius)

anchor.snippet: The fact that the Frobenius map is surjective

annotations.note: `PerfectRing.ofFiniteOfIsReduced` (and the field-level `PerfectField.ofFinite`) deduce perfection from surjective Frobenius.

annotations.label: Irreducible polynomial over a finite field

anchor.snippet: a non-constant monic polynomial with coefficients in

annotations.note: The general `Irreducible` predicate applies to `Polynomial K`; no finite-field-specific definition is needed.

annotations.label: Unique factorization of monic polynomials over finite fields

anchor.snippet: every polynomial ring over a field is a unique factorization domain

annotations.note: Polynomial rings over UFDs are UFDs by the global instance in `Mathlib/RingTheory/Polynomial/UniqueFactorization.lean`, which applies to fields.

annotations.label: Finite fields are not algebraically closed

anchor.snippet: is not algebraically closed

annotations.note: No direct statement `[Finite K] [Field K] → ¬ IsAlgClosed K` was found.

annotations.label: Algebraic closure of GF(p) and its structure

anchor.snippet: be an algebraic closure of

annotations.note: `AlgebraicClosure (ZMod p)` exists generically; structural details specific to finite-field closures are not separately stated.

annotations.label: Unique degree-n subextension of the algebraic closure

anchor.snippet: it is the unique degree n extension

annotations.note: No statement isolating, inside `AlgebraicClosure (ZMod p)`, the unique subfield of degree `n` was found.

annotations.label: Algebraic closure as direct limit

anchor.snippet: Any algebraic extension is the union of its finite subextensions

annotations.note: No realization of `AlgebraicClosure (ZMod p)` as the direct limit `colim GaloisField p n` was found.

anchors.snippet: Any algebraic extension is the union of its finite subextensions

annotations.label: Wedderburn's little theorem

anchor.snippet: Wedderburn's little theorem states that all finite division rings are commutative

annotations.note: `littleWedderburn` provides a `Field` instance for any finite division ring.

annotations.label: Artin–Zorn theorem

anchor.snippet: The Artin–Zorn theorem states that all alternative division rings are finite fields

annotations.note: Artin–Zorn is explicitly listed as future work in the header of `Mathlib/RingTheory/LittleWedderburn.lean` and is not formalized.
